nodes.label: Laravel Best Practices Framework
nodes.author: laravel
nodes.rationale: Provides prioritized Laravel defaults while requiring established project conventions to take precedence.

nodes.label: Advanced Query Patterns
nodes.rationale: Use index-friendly subqueries, selective aggregates, and correlated ordering to reduce query count and avoid row duplication.

nodes.label: Architecture Best Practices
nodes.rationale: Favor focused actions, constructor injection, contracts, conventions, and appropriate concurrency mechanisms.

nodes.label: Blade and Views Best Practices
nodes.rationale: Use explicit component interfaces and centralized view data to avoid hidden coupling and duplicate rendering behavior.

nodes.label: Caching Best Practices
nodes.rationale: Use framework cache primitives for coherent cache-aside behavior, safe invalidation, request memoization, and race prevention.

nodes.label: Collections Best Practices
nodes.rationale: Choose collection and lazy iteration APIs according to memory, relationship loading, and mutation needs.

nodes.label: Configuration Best Practices
nodes.rationale: Keep environment access in configuration and centralize reusable values instead of hardcoding runtime settings.

nodes.label: Database Performance Best Practices
nodes.rationale: Prevent N+1 queries and control memory and query cost through eager loading, focused selects, indexes, and batch processing.

nodes.label: Eloquent Best Practices
nodes.rationale: Model relationships, casts, and reusable scopes should make data access explicit and maintainable.

nodes.label: Error Handling Best Practices
nodes.rationale: Centralize reporting and rendering while preserving structured context and protecting log systems from noisy exceptions.

nodes.label: Events and Notifications Best Practices
nodes.rationale: Use discovered events and transaction-aware, queued notifications for reliable asynchronous side effects.

nodes.label: HTTP Client Best Practices
nodes.rationale: External requests require explicit timeouts, retries, response checks, concurrency where suitable, and deterministic fakes in tests.

nodes.label: Mail Best Practices
nodes.rationale: Use queued Markdown mailables and test their queueing behavior to keep transactional mail reliable.

nodes.label: Migrations Best Practices
nodes.rationale: Schema changes must be generated, indexed, reversible where possible, production-safe, and limited to one concern.

nodes.label: Queue Jobs Best Practices
nodes.rationale: Jobs need aligned timeouts and retries, duplicate protection, failure handling, rate limits, and batching for reliable background work.

nodes.label: Routing and Controllers Best Practices
nodes.rationale: Use resource routes, scoped model binding, auto-validating requests, and small controller methods delegated to actions or services.

nodes.label: Task Scheduling Best Practices
nodes.rationale: Scheduled work should avoid overlapping, coordinate across servers, respect environments, and bound long-running processing.

nodes.label: Security Best Practices
nodes.rationale: Protect mass assignment, authorization, SQL and XSS boundaries, CSRF, uploads, secrets, dependencies, and sensitive persistence.

nodes.label: Conventions and Style
nodes.rationale: Laravel naming and helper conventions make code concise, UTF-8-safe, and easier to understand without comments.

nodes.label: Testing Best Practices
nodes.rationale: Use efficient database refreshes, expressive model assertions, factory states, correctly timed fakes, and recycled related models.

nodes.label: Validation and Forms Best Practices
nodes.rationale: Dedicated form requests and validated input constrain mass operations and make conditional or cross-field validation explicit.

nodes.label: Laravel Best Practices Framework Mirror
nodes.author: laravel
nodes.rationale: Mirrors the Laravel best-practices framework for the Claude skill location.
